items.front: List all 10 clearance items in 4-2-1 order (a through j)
items.back: (a) Aircraft ID, (b) Clearance limit, (c) SID or vectors, (d) Route of flight, (e) Altitude data, (f) Mach number, (g) USAF transmission limit, (h) Holding, (i) Special info, (j) Frequency and beacon code.

items.front: USAF airborne altitude transmission: which 5 items are limited to one per transmission?
items.back: 1. Frequency change. 2. Transponder change. 3. Heading. 4. Altimeter setting. 5. Traffic information containing an altitude.

items.front: Clearance limit phrase for an intersection or waypoint when type is known
items.back: Cleared to (intersection or waypoint name and type). Example: 'Cleared to HANEY intersection.'

items.front: What phraseology keeps SID altitude restrictions when changing only top altitude?
items.back: Climb via SID except maintain (new top altitude). Example: 'Climb via SID except maintain Flight Level Two Three Zero.'

items.front: Can ATC cancel or amend ODP crossing altitudes and speed restrictions?
items.back: No. Crossing altitudes and speed restrictions on Obstacle Departure Procedures cannot be canceled or amended by ATC.

items.front: What happens if 'maintain' altitude is restated and prior altitude restrictions are omitted?
items.back: Those previously issued altitude restrictions are canceled, including SID/STAR altitude restrictions if any.

items.front: NRP aircraft: what should be done after tactical conflict resolution?
items.back: Make every effort to return the aircraft to the original filed flight plan and altitude as soon as conditions warrant.
